assumptions: The declared intragroup dividend has created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable/payable.

The receivable/payable is between group entities with different functional currencies, so the related foreign exchange gains or losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation in accordance with IAS 21.
Any hedge designation would also satisfy the formal documentation and effectiveness requirements in IFRS 9 paragraph 6.4.1.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: A recorded intragroup dividend receivable/payable is a recognised asset or liability, so fair value hedge accounting is a distinct hedge model to consider if the intragroup monetary item exception applies in consolidation.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 separately provides for cash flow hedge accounting for a recognised asset or liability exposed to a particular risk, making it another distinct hedge accounting model relevant to the foreign currency exposure.
